title: 7 Herbal Essential Oils For Itchy Scalp
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a game-changer for an itchy scalp. These natural remedies work by soothing the skin and reducing inflammation, which is often the root cause of itchiness. For example, Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can lead to itchiness. Eucalyptus globulus oil, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp.  Herbal teas can also help alleviate an itchy scalp. Drinking teas made from certain herbs can help promote a healthy scalp from the inside out. For instance, Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary tea, has antioxidant properties that can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. This can lead to a healthier scalp that is less prone to itchiness. Additionally, rosemary tea has been known to improve circulation, which can help bring oxygen and nutrients to the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.  Using herbal essential oils and drinking herbal teas can bring numerous benefits to your life. For one, it can help you avoid harsh chemical-based treatments that can damage your hair and scalp. It can also help you identify and address underlying issues that may be contributing to your itchy scalp, such as fungal or bacterial infections. By incorporating herbal essential oils and teas into your routine, you can take a proactive approach to maintaining a healthy scalp and promoting overall well-being.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains active constituents like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and limonene. These compounds have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp infections and inflammation. The antiseptic properties of these constituents help reduce redness and irritation, while also promoting a healthy scalp environment. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and discomfort. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils may help alleviate itchy scalp conditions by addressing the underlying causes of irritation.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, 2 cups of water, and a pot.
Combine the Melaleuca leaves and water in the pot, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Use a hydrosol distiller or a still to extract the essential oil from the mixture, then store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains cineole and alpha-pinene as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp. Cineole, in particular, has a cooling effect that can help to reduce redness and irritation. Alpha-pinene, on the other hand, has antimicrobial properties that can help to control the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that can contribute to an itchy scalp. By using eucalyptus globulus essential oils, you can help to create an environment that is less conducive to irritation and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of water in a large saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Eucalyptus leaves. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the solids to get all the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a still or a distillation apparatus and distill to separate the essential oil from the water.
Collect the essential oil in a separate container, it should be a clear, colorless liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains a compound called borneol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. This oil also contains camphor, which has a cooling effect that can soothe an itchy scalp. The antioxidant properties of 1,8-cineole, another compound found in this essential oil, help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial properties of camphor and borneol help control the growth of yeast and bacteria that can contribute to an itchy scalp. The antiseptic properties of this essential oil also help prevent infections and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Wash the leaves with water, then pat them dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.
Combine the leaves and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops on your itchy scalp as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains active constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-juice. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm an itchy scalp. Aloe-emodin has antifungal properties, which can help control fungal infections that cause scalp irritation. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of aloe barbadensis essential oils work together to reduce redness and itching on the scalp. The oil's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the scalp, providing relief from dryness and itchiness.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves, a cup of coconut oil, and a glass jar with a lid.
Wash the Aloe leaves with water, then dry them with a clean towel.
In a blender, mix the Aloe leaves with 1/2 cup of coconut oil until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to the glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new jar. Your Aloe essential oil is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains a compound called linalool, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, making it a popular remedy for itchy scalps. Linalyl acetate, another key constituent of lavender oil, has a calming effect on the skin and can help to soothe irritation. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of lavender oil also help to control the growth of yeast and bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness. By reducing inflammation, calming the skin, and controlling the growth of microorganisms, lavender oil can help to alleviate itchy scalp issues.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and a clean glass jar.
Place 1/4 cup of dried flowers in a clean glass bowl and set aside 1/4 cup for later use.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the bowl with the dried flowers.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the bowl daily, then strain the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Discard the solids and transfer the Lavandula angustifolia essential oil to a clean glass bottle for use on itchy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains a high concentration of citral, limonene, and beta-pinene, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp, reducing redness and irritation. The antiseptic properties of citral and limonene also help to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to scalp irritation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of beta-pinene help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the scalp, providing relief from itchy sensations. By targeting the underlying causes of an itchy scalp, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can help to promote a healthy scalp and reduce the symptoms of itchiness.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for 2 hours. This allows the leaves to release their oils.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the leaves.
Use a hydrosol kit or a still to distill the liquid and separate the essential oil from the water. You will get about 1 tablespoon of essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp by reducing redness and irritation. Thymol has a decongestant effect, which can help to clear out pores and reduce inflammation on the scalp. Carvacrol has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy skin. By using Thymus vulgaris essential oils, you can help to create an environment that's less conducive to itchy scalp conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to the jar and mix well.
Place the jar in a saucepan with 2 cups of water and heat it on low heat for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, turn off the heat and let the mixture cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar.
Store the Thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops on itchy scalp 2-3 times a week.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with an itchy scalp. Some teas, like those with peppermint or chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritations. Others, like tea tree oil-infused tea, have antifungal properties that can help control scalp infections. Drinking tea may help reduce itchiness and promote a healthy scalp.
faq_safe: You can try consuming herbal teas for an itchy scalp, but do it in moderation. Some herbal teas, like those with peppermint or chamomile, might help soothe the scalp. However, drinking them every day could interact with other medications or have side effects, so it's best to limit your intake to a few cups a week.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few weeks to show results for an itchy scalp. Some people notice improvement within a week, while others may not see changes until two to three weeks. It's essential to be patient and consistent with your herbal tea routine to give your scalp time to adjust and start feeling better.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for an itchy scalp can be soothing. Try sipping a warm cup in the morning after waking up or in the evening before bed. The calming effects of the tea can help ease itchiness and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep or start the day feeling more comfortable.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, even if they're for an itchy scalp. Some herbal teas, like those containing sage or peppermint, may affect how your body processes other medications. If you're taking prescription meds, be cautious when drinking herbal teas to avoid unexpected side effects or changes in how your medication works.
faq_pregnant: If you have an itchy scalp during pregnancy, you might consider drinking herbal teas. However, some herbs can be strong and may affect your baby. Peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered safe, but it's still a good idea to check the ingredients and make sure they're not too potent.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and have an itchy scalp, you can try herbal teas like peppermint or chamomile. They might help soothe and calm the itch. However, some herbs like sage and pennyroyal can pass into breast milk, so it's best to choose ones that are considered safe for breastfeeding mothers.
